mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various agent frameworks. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and the potential for increased resource consumption due to its comprehensive features.

steps.title: Add the Kagenti Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Kagenti Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Kagenti easily.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official Kagenti Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kagenti CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Kagenti to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.4.0-alpha.1/deploy/crds/kagenti.io_kagentis.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Kagenti using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.4.0-alpha.1

```

> This installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to demonstrate how to use Kagenti.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: Kagent
metadata:
  name: example-kagent
spec:
  image: kagenti/kagent:latest
EOF

```

> This creates a sample Kagent resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: Kagent
metadata:
  name: example-kagent
spec:
  image: kagenti/kagent:latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Kagent is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Kagent to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kagent example-kagent -o yaml

```

> This command retrieves the details of the Kagent resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get kagent example-kagent -o yaml > kagent-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.4.0-alpha.1

kubectl get kagent example-kagent -o yaml

```

> Backup the current Kagent configuration.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that the Kagent is still running correctly after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kagent example-kagent -o yaml > kagent-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kagent not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kagent resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Kagent pod using 'kubectl logs <kagent-pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart may not be available or the repository may not be added correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Helm repository is added correctly and run 'helm repo update'.

troubleshooting.title: Kagent is in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources in the cluster to schedule the Kagent pod.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in the Kagent spec and ensure your cluster has enough resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kagent service may not be running or exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service and pod status using 'kubectl get svc' and 'kubectl get pods' to ensure they are running.

versionNotes.changes: Initial release with support for Kagent deployment and management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kagent resource in the 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get kagent'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: Kagent
metadata:
  name: example-kagent
spec:
  image: kagenti/kagent:latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
